summary: Matched the Home-Run Contest stage function grHomeRun_8021ED74 by replacing its undecompiled placeholder with a small Bob-Omb rain setup routine. The function now initializes a BobOmbRain struct, obtains a stage JObj with Ground_801C2CF4(0x7F), reads its position into the struct via lb_8000B1CC, and spawns/starts the item effect with it_8026BE84. The header prototype was tightened from unknown return/params to void grHomeRun_8021ED74(void). PR body reports a clean ninja build and fuzzy_match_percent 100.0.
what_changed: Added #include "it/it_26B1.h" to grhomerun.c so the stage code can use BobOmbRain and it_8026BE84.
Implemented grHomeRun_8021ED74 as a void function instead of leaving it as an undecompiled placeholder.
Created a local BobOmbRain struct and initialized x0 and x4 to NULL, x14 to 0xB, x18 to 0, and x1C.b0 to 1 before dispatch.
Fetched an HSD_JObj from Ground_801C2CF4(0x7F), populated bobomb_rain.x8_vec with lb_8000B1CC(jobj, NULL, &bobomb_rain.x8_vec), then called it_8026BE84(&bobomb_rain).
Updated grhomerun.h prototype from UNK_RET grHomeRun_8021ED74(UNK_PARAMS) to void grHomeRun_8021ED74(void).
smart_moves: Used the existing item header rather than re-declaring the BobOmbRain struct or item spawn routine locally.
Tightened the public header signature once the function body made it clear there are no parameters and no return value.
Kept the implementation minimal and matched around known engine helpers: Ground_801C2CF4 for stage object lookup, lb_8000B1CC for vector extraction, and it_8026BE84 for Bob-Omb rain handling.
Explicitly initialized the local struct fields used by the item call, which is important for matching stack layout and avoiding uninitialized-field ambiguity.
decomp_lessons: Small stage functions may simply prepare an item/effect parameter struct and hand it to an item subsystem routine; look for nearby item headers before inventing local types.
When a previously unknown prototype becomes obvious from the matched function body, update the header immediately from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS to the real signature.
Position-based stage effects often use a stage JObj lookup followed by lb_8000B1CC into a Vec3 field before spawning an item or effect.
Preserve evidence for unexplained constants such as 0x7F and 0xB rather than overnaming them without broader context.
naming_conventions: Address-based stage symbol grHomeRun_8021ED74 was retained; no semantic rename was introduced in this PR.
Existing project type names were reused: BobOmbRain, HSD_JObj, Vec3-style x8_vec field.
Unknown struct members stayed offset-style, such as x0, x4, x14, x18, x1C.b0, consistent with partially understood decomp structs.
Header comments retained the address marker format, e.g. /* 21ED74 */.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Converted the function to void(void), likely matching a no-argument/no-return call pattern.
Used a local BobOmbRain stack struct with direct field assignments before passing its address to it_8026BE84.
Maintained exact constants visible in the matching code: Ground_801C2CF4(0x7F) and bobomb_rain.x14 = 0xB.
Used NULL assignments and helper-call ordering in a straightforward sequence that produced a reported 100.0 fuzzy match.
review_feedback: No review comments were present in the provided PR context.
PR body verification states that ninja builds clean and fuzzy_match_percent is 100.0.
follow_up_queries: Find other grhomerun functions that call it_8026BE84 or initialize BobOmbRain.
Investigate what stage object or joint ID 0x7F represents in Home-Run Contest.
Investigate the semantic meaning of BobOmbRain.x14 value 0xB and x1C.b0.
Check neighboring functions grHomeRun_8021EC58 and grHomeRun_8021EDD4 for related stage event or item-spawn behavior.
